Tests the default string representation of the Number prototype in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the default behavior of the `toString` method on the `Number` prototype in JavaScript. It verifies that calling `toString` on `Number.prototype` returns the string "0". This test is likely used to ensure that the `toString` method behaves as expected in the JavaScript environment being used. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of the `Number` prototype's behavior.
